title: Remove the Tipper Pneumatic Cylinder
summary: Safely remove the pneumatic cylinder from the tipper by locking out the equipment, gaining access to the cylinder, disconnecting the air lines, and releasing the cylinder from its rod-end and lower-bracket mounting points.
when_to_use: Use when the pneumatic cylinder must be removed from the tipper assembly as described in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual section for pneumatic cylinder removal.
not_for: Do not use this as an operator procedure; the source-backed candidate indicates this task should be handled by L2 support because it includes LOTO, guarded/internal access, pneumatic disconnection, and mechanical component removal.
Do not use when source-supported access, tools, or lockout/tagout controls are unavailable.

responsibilities: Perform lockout/tagout using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Remove access panels and covers as needed.
Disconnect air lines and release cylinder mounting hardware.
Use caution when releasing the rod-end connection because the lift plate will drop a short distance.
Escalate if the cylinder or associated hardware cannot be removed as described.

safety_notes: Perform lockout/tagout before beginning work using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Use caution when releasing the rod-end connection because the lift plate will drop a short distance.
Stop and escalate if the cylinder, retaining ring, pin, air lines, bracket, or fasteners cannot be removed as described by the source.

access_required: Access to the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure
system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out before removal work begins.
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2 of the referenced source material.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-10 Pneumatic Cylinder; use for cylinder location, access area, and removal context.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe isolated state before accessing and removing the pneumatic cylinder.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2 before performing any removal work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is isolated and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: Lockout/tagout has been applied in accordance with the referenced procedure before any covers, panels, air lines, or mounting hardware are removed.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out/tagged out or cannot be placed in the required safe state.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
The equipment cannot be confirmed in a safe isolated state.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if access is needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the pneumatic cylinder area.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the area.
steps.expected_result: The side panel is removed when needed and the cylinder access area is exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: The station side panel is removed without damage and access to the cylinder area is available.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel or fasteners cannot be removed as described, or access to the cylinder area remains obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove station side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pneumatic cylinder location and surrounding access area associated with Figure 7-10.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed as described.
Fasteners cannot be removed with the specified tool.
Access to the cylinder area is still not available after panel removal.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the area.
Nearby procedure steps instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove nine self-tapping fasteners from the side panel if needed...

steps.title: Remove the gripper cover
steps.purpose: Expose the pneumatic cylinder and related connections for removal.
steps.instruction: Remove the gripper cover by removing the eight M8 button-head screws.
steps.expected_result: The gripper cover is removed and the cylinder area is further exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: All eight M8 button-head screws are removed and the gripper cover is off without damage.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper cover or its screws cannot be removed as described.

actions.label: Remove gripper cover screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the eight M8 button-head screws.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pneumatic cylinder removal context and nearby cover/access area associated with Figure 7-10.

stop_or_escalate_if: The gripper cover cannot be removed as described.
The M8 button-head screws cannot be removed.

source_evidence.text: Remove the gripper cover by removing the eight M8 button-head screws.
Nearby procedure steps instruct the technician to ... remove the gripper cover by taking out eight M8 button head screws.

steps.title: Disconnect the air lines from the cylinder
steps.purpose: Free the pneumatic cylinder from its air connections before mechanical release.
steps.instruction: Remove the two air lines from the pneumatic cylinder.
steps.expected_result: Both air lines are disconnected from the cylinder.
steps.healthy_condition: The two air lines are removed from the pneumatic cylinder and no remaining air-line connection prevents cylinder removal.
steps.failure_condition: One or both air lines cannot be removed as described.

actions.label: Remove air lines
actions.exact_action: Remove the two air lines from the pneumatic cylinder.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pneumatic cylinder identification and air-line connection area associated with Figure 7-10.

stop_or_escalate_if: The air lines cannot be disconnected as described.
The cylinder remains connected by air lines after attempted removal.

source_evidence.text: Remove the two air lines from the pneumatic cylinder.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.

steps.title: Release the rod-end connection
steps.purpose: Disconnect the cylinder from the lift plate at the rod end.
steps.instruction: At the rod end of the cylinder, remove one retaining ring and pull the pin to release the lift plate. Use caution because the lift plate will drop a short distance.
steps.expected_result: The rod end is disconnected from the lift plate.
steps.healthy_condition: The retaining ring and pin are removed and the lift plate is released as expected, dropping only a short distance.
steps.failure_condition: The retaining ring or pin cannot be removed, or the rod-end connection cannot be released as described.

actions.label: Remove retaining ring
actions.exact_action: Remove one retaining ring at the rod end of the cylinder.

actions.label: Pull rod-end pin
actions.exact_action: Pull the pin to release the lift plate.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Rod-end area of the pneumatic cylinder and associated mounting/release context from Figure 7-10.

stop_or_escalate_if: The retaining ring cannot be removed.
The rod-end connection cannot be released as described.
The lift plate behavior is not as expected when released.

source_evidence.text: At the rod end of the cylinder, remove one retaining ring and pull the pin to release the lift plate; note that the lift plate will drop a short distance.
Use caution when releasing the rod-end connection because the source states the lift plate will drop a short distance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.

steps.title: Release the lower bracket and free the cylinder
steps.purpose: Remove the remaining lower mounting hardware so the air cylinder is free from the tipper assembly.
steps.instruction: At the bottom of the cylinder, remove the two M6 socket head screws to release half of the lower bracket and free the air cylinder.
steps.expected_result: The lower bracket is released and the air cylinder is free for removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The two M6 socket head screws are removed, half of the lower bracket is released, and the air cylinder is free from its mounting points.
steps.failure_condition: The lower bracket or M6 screws cannot be removed as described, or the cylinder remains captured after bracket release.

actions.label: Remove lower bracket screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the two M6 socket head screws at the bottom of the cylinder.

actions.label: Release lower bracket
actions.exact_action: Release half of the lower bracket and free the air cylinder.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Bottom of the pneumatic cylinder and lower bracket area associated with Figure 7-10.

stop_or_escalate_if: The two M6 socket head screws cannot be removed.
The lower bracket cannot be released as described.
The air cylinder is not free after the bracket is released.

source_evidence.text: At the bottom of the cylinder, remove the two M6 socket head screws to release half of the lower bracket and free the air cylinder.
Escalate if the cylinder, retaining ring, pin, air lines, bracket, or fasteners cannot be removed as described by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.

success_criteria: The pneumatic cylinder is freed from its air connections and mounting points and removed from the tipper assembly.
The tipper was locked out/tagged out before removal work.
The side panel and gripper cover were removed as needed to gain access.
The two air lines were disconnected from the cylinder.
The rod-end retaining ring and pin were removed and the lift plate released.
The lower bracket was released by removing the two M6 socket head screws.
healthy_conditions: The tipper is safely isolated under the referenced lockout/tagout procedure.
Access panels and covers are removed as needed without preventing further work.
The cylinder is disconnected from both air lines and both mounting locations.
The lift plate drops only a short distance when the rod-end connection is released, consistent with the source note.
The air cylinder is free from the assembly at the end of the procedure.
failure_conditions: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed or confirmed.
The side panel, gripper cover, air lines, retaining ring, pin, lower bracket, or fasteners cannot be removed as described.
The lift plate behavior during rod-end release is not as expected.
The cylinder remains captured after the documented removal steps are completed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate to appropriate support if lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
Escalate if the cylinder, retaining ring, pin, air lines, bracket, or fasteners cannot be removed as described by the source.
Because this procedure includes LOTO and component removal, it should be handled by L2 support rather than an operator.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall pneumatic cylinder location, access context, and removal reference from Figure 7-10.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-10 appears on page 148 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_10_1_pneumatic_cylinder_removal.
